$schema_note: M5 worked + contrast transcripts. Multi-stakeholder (founder + finance) synthesized from §10.2 L.D. Brex/FX pattern (GC-06) extended per storyboard. Anonymised initials. No PII.

worked.title: Brex displacement with FX hook, founder + finance lead
worked.sme_pattern: L.D. §10.2 (Berlin) — 'never argue against Brex, ask about FX exposure'

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Tom — I saw you closed Series-B last quarter. Who handles per-employee card issuance now that you're hiring fast?

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: Honestly it's a mess. I'm Slack-pinging finance every time someone joins. We already use Brex though.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: So issuance is the bottleneck — Brex sits on top but you're still the human approval each time.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Quick question — what does your FX exposure on EUR-USD SaaS subscriptions look like?

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: Honestly? No idea. S.K. would know — she's the finance lead. Let me grab her.

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: Hi — Tom briefed me. We use Brex, and our FX spread on Anthropic + AWS in USD is brutal — about 2.4% on our card statements.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: S.K., before I go anywhere — what's the monthly EUR-USD volume you're moving on supplier payments?

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: About €60K a month on SaaS + €20K on contractors. So 2.4% is ~€1,900 a month I'm leaking.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Got it — so the actual leak is the spread, not the issuance speed Tom flagged.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: We run at interbank on EUR-USD, no markup. On €80K/month you'd recover roughly €1,800 — and Tom gets per-employee cards issued in 2 minutes via our API.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: That's the actual number I wanted.

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: And we can keep Brex for US-only spend?

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Yes — additive, not replacement. Tom, S.K. — Tuesday 11 or Thursday 14 for a 20-min FX deep-dive with both of you?

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: Thursday works.

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: Send it to both of us.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: [Sends calendar invite · tom@startup.example + sarah@startup.example · Salesforce Next_Step_Booked__c = TRUE]

contrast.title: Contrast — founder only / SDR didn't redo M1 → call lost
contrast.sme_pattern: Inverts §10.4 P.B. pattern + storyboard failure-mode for multi-stakeholder.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Hi Tom — wanted to walk you through how we're cheaper than Brex on FX.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: We're locked into Brex this year.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: Our fees are still lower though — and our API for issuance is faster.

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: Sorry, what's the call about?

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: As I was telling Tom, our card is cheaper than Brex with a faster API — let me show you the spend-control platform.

turns.speaker: S.K.
turns.line: We don't have a spend-control problem.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.line: Yeah — we need to drop. Send pricing if you've got it.

turns.speaker: Rep
turns.line: [Hangs up · no invite sent · Salesforce Next_Step_Booked__c = FALSE]

contrast.failure_pattern: Rep pitched Tom's frame at S.K. without firing a fresh M1 for the new stakeholder. S.K.'s actual pain (FX spread on supplier payments) never surfaced. Jargon token 'spend-control platform' flagged on line 0:34 (brief §18.1 violation).
